name: Kung Pao Chicken #1
recipeInstructions:
1. Dice chicken and soak in cornstarch, soy sauce mixture for at least 1/2 hour.
2. Clean chiles of seeds, cut into 1-inch pieces; set aside.
3. Fry peanuts in a bit of oil until just golden (do not burn). Set aside.
4. Stir fry chicken for only about 1/2 minute; set aside and discard the oil.
5. Make up a seasoning sauce with 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 2 tablespoons white wine, 2 tablespoons sugar, 1 tablespoon cornstarch, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1 tablespoon sesame oil.
6. In 2 tablespoons oil, fry chiles until black, then add ginger and the chicken. Continue stirring, and add the seasoning sauce. Stir until sauce thickens slightly and everything is well heated.
7. Remove from heat and mix in peanuts.
Adjust the amount of red chiles to taste; if you have low tolerance for heat, reduce by half your first time. You may also adapt the recipe for shrimp or beef.
